title: Patch Express path-to-regexp ReDoS vulnerability (GHSA-37ch-88jc-xwx2)
description: Express 4.21.2 ships with path-to-regexp@0.1.12 which has a confirmed ReDoS (CVSS 7.5). Routes /api/campaigns/:id/images and /api/content/:page use :param syntax with the vulnerable version. A crafted URL with a long repeated-character path segment can hang the Node.js event loop indefinitely. The API is publicly accessible with no authentication, making this a real DoS vector. Fix: upgrade to express@^5 or add an npm overrides entry in package.json to pin path-to-regexp to a patched version. Also add request-level timeout middleware as defense-in-depth.

title: Upgrade DOMPurify from 3.3.1 to 3.4.0 to patch XSS CVEs
description: DOMPurify 3.3.1 has 7 known CVEs including two with direct XSS impact: GHSA-crv5-9vww-q3g8 (SAFE_FOR_TEMPLATES bypass, CVSS 6.8), GHSA-v9jr-rg53-9pgp (prototype pollution to XSS via CUSTOM_ELEMENT_HANDLING, CVSS 6.9), and GHSA-h8r8-wccr-v5f2 (mutation-XSS via re-contextualization). The app uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML with DOMPurify-sanitized server content (App.tsx line 315, ContentModal.tsx line 74). If any bypass works, arbitrary script execution is possible. Run: npm install dompurify@^3.4.0 — the DOMPurify.sanitize API is unchanged.

title: Surface blob fetch failures to client
description: server/server.ts:105-126 — when blob API fails and cache is empty, server returns an empty list. Client shows 'no images' instead of 'couldn't load'. Return 503 (or pass through 429) so the UI can render an error state and offer retry.

title: Require explicit campaign id in campaigns.yaml
description: server/server.ts:135 falls back to Math.random().toString(36) when neither hashtag nor icon_path is present. Non-deterministic slugs break hash-URL deep linking across reloads. Require an explicit 'id' field in data/campaigns.yaml and error at startup if missing.

title: Add TTL and size cap to server content cache
description: server/server.ts:85 — contentCache.set() has no TTL or size limit. Memory grows unbounded and stale content is never invalidated. Reuse the 30s-TTL pattern already used for blob listings; cap entry count.

title: Gate BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN log behind IS_DEV
description: server/server.ts:63 logs token presence unconditionally. Safe in dev, risky if prod logs are exposed. Only log presence/absence when IS_DEV is true.

title: Add timeout to image preload
description: client/src/App.tsx:169-184 — Image.src has no timeout, so a slow/dead CDN response freezes the loading state indefinitely. Wrap each preload in Promise.race with a timeout and surface a recoverable error in the UI.

title: Fix content modal load race
description: client/src/App.tsx:269-303 — activeModalRef check is fragile under slow network. If user opens modal A, then B before A loads, A's content can clobber B. Use AbortController or a version counter pattern (same fix shape as the campaign-load race).

title: Fix race condition in campaign image loading
description: client/src/App.tsx:123-210 — rapid campaign switching can resolve stale promises into state because isCampaignLoadingRef only gates new loads but doesn't cancel in-flight ones. Add AbortController or version token so only the latest selection's results are applied.

title: Add React error boundary around app shell
description: Currently any uncaught render error crashes the entire gallery (client/src/App.tsx). Add an ErrorBoundary component with a graceful fallback UI and wrap the top-level tree.

title: Split App.tsx into focused components
description: Extract from client/src/App.tsx (~1100 lines): <CampaignNavigation>, <LightboxContainer>, <ContentModal>, <LoadingManager>. Co-locate related state. Reduces stale-closure risk and clarifies ownership of state.

title: Epic: Server hardening
description: server/server.ts has several risky patterns: token presence logged unconditionally, unbounded content cache, non-deterministic campaign slug fallback, swallowed blob errors, handrolled .env parser, no input validation. Tighten the production code path.

title: Epic: Decompose monolithic App.tsx
description: App.tsx is ~1100 lines with 20+ useState calls, mixing toolbar, sidebar, lightbox, modals, settings, campaign selection, and loading screen logic. This causes stale-closure bugs, race conditions, and makes the code hard to reason about. Break into smaller components and add an error boundary.

title: Add e2e tests for Settings modal: open/close, toggles, and localStorage persistence
description: No e2e or unit tests cover the Settings modal. The lightbox-redesign.spec.ts reduce-effects test bypasses Settings entirely by setting the class via page.evaluate. The modal is critical path — it controls every animation in the app. Add an e2e test in app.spec.ts that: opens the Settings modal, toggles Reduce Effects on, asserts document.documentElement.classList.contains('reduce-effects'), reloads, asserts the class persists from localStorage. Also test: focus trap (Tab stays inside modal), Escape closes the modal, toggling Reduce Motion.

title: Reset campaign progress and error state on cache-hit path in selectCampaign
description: In App.tsx (lines 210-233), when selectCampaign detects a cache hit (line 226), it calls setIsCampaignLoading(false) but does not reset setCampaignLoadProgress, setCampaignLoadTotal, or setCampaignLoadError. If the previous campaign had load errors (campaignLoadError: true) or partial progress counts, those stale values persist when the user navigates back to the cached campaign. The error banner 'Some images failed to load' can appear for a campaign that loaded successfully. Fix: reset all campaign load state (progress, total, error) in the cache-hit branch before the early return.

title: Fix App.test.js to test actual behavior instead of reimplementing production logic
description: client/test/App.test.js tests reconstruct internal functions (createCloseAllSubwindows, placeholder creation) as local variables inside the test file and assert on them without importing or rendering App.tsx. The test's createCloseAllSubwindows at line 103 is already out of sync: the real closeAllSubwindows in App.tsx (line 464) closes isSettingsOpen, but the test copy does not include it. Any new subwindow requires updating both the production code and the manually-maintained test copy — the tests won't enforce this. Fix: either use @testing-library/react to render App and test actual behavior, or extract closeAllSubwindows to a utility function that can be directly imported and tested.

title: Decouple loadingDots animation from isPreloading — dots are frozen on campaign-switch progress bar
description: In App.tsx, the loadingDots setInterval (lines 444-450) is driven by isPreloading. After initial load isPreloading becomes false, stopping the interval. But loadingDots is also rendered in the per-campaign progress bar that appears during campaign switches (line 600, controlled by isCampaignLoading). On a campaign switch, the dot animation is visually frozen at whatever character the interval last produced. Fix: either drive the dot animation from isCampaignLoading (the flag that actually controls the progress bar visibility) or remove the animated dots from the campaign-switch progress bar entirely.

title: Extend validate-config.js to cover theme and lightbox.sidebarAnimation config sections
description: scripts/validate-config.js uses a hand-rolled YAML parser that only handles the loadingScreen section (line 48-49 only sets currentSection for 'loadingScreen:'). theme.accentColor and all lightbox.sidebarAnimation fields are silently ignored at build time. Invalid values (bad color, negative timing) produce a console.warn at runtime that is easily missed. Fix: replace the hand-rolled parser with js-yaml (already in package.json) and add validation rules for theme.accentColor and each lightbox.sidebarAnimation field. Ideally share validation logic with client/src/config/.

title: Add HTTP Cache-Control headers to /api/campaigns and /api/build-info responses
description: GET /api/campaigns returns static data that only changes on deploy, but the response has no Cache-Control header. Every page load hits the server and triggers a synchronous YAML read. GET /api/build-info is immutable for the process lifetime. Both endpoints should serve Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600, stale-while-revalidate=86400 (or similar) to allow CDN/browser caching and eliminate redundant server hits for returning users.

title: Fix FringeGlyphLoadingSpinner setInterval leak when component unmounts before displayDuration
description: In FringeGlyphLoadingSpinner.tsx lines 189-210, the display timer creates a setTimeout that spawns a setInterval inside its callback. The interval variable is local to the effect body and is null at cleanup time if the component unmounts before displayDuration elapses. clearInterval(null) is a no-op, so the interval continues firing after unmount and calls advanceToNextImage in a loop. mountedRef prevents the state update crash but the interval itself keeps running. Fix: store the interval ID in a separate intervalRef so the cleanup function can always cancel it regardless of whether the timeout has fired.

title: Fix imagesByCampaign cache to store enriched images instead of raw API data
description: In App.tsx, imagesByCampaign (line 72) is populated with raw campaignImages (line 199, ApiImageData[]) instead of fullyLoadedImages (line 192, ImageData[] with isLoading:false and loadedSrc set). When a cached campaign is re-selected (line 226-229), setImages receives ApiImageData[] objects missing loadedSrc and isLoading, so img.loadedSrc is undefined for the entire gallery. Fix: change line 199 from setImagesByCampaign(prev => ({...prev, [id]: campaignImages})) to use fullyLoadedImages instead. Both the performance reviewer (cache stores stale objects) and maintainability reviewer (TypeScript silent mismatch) flagged this.

title: Cache campaigns.yaml parse at server startup instead of re-reading on every request
description: loadCampaigns() calls fs.readFileSync and yaml.load synchronously on every request to both GET /api/campaigns and GET /api/campaigns/:id/images (server.ts lines 264-291, 371, 381). This is a blocking FS read + YAML parse + duplicate-ID sweep on every hot-path request. The data never changes during process lifetime. Fix: parse campaigns once at startup into a module-level variable; expose a getCampaigns() function with memoization that both routes call. The existing blobCache pattern in server.ts already models this.

title: Upgrade Vite dev server from 7.1.3 to 7.4.0 to patch filesystem CVEs
description: Vite 7.1.3 has 6 CVEs in the 7.0.0-7.3.1 range: GHSA-p9ff-h696-f583 (arbitrary file read via WebSocket, High), GHSA-v2wj-q39q-566r (server.fs.deny bypass, High), GHSA-4w7w-66w2-5vf9 (path traversal in optimized deps, Moderate). These only affect npm run dev, not the production Express server, but anyone running the dev server with the Vite port network-accessible exposes their local filesystem. Fix: npm install --save-dev vite@^7.4.0

title: Add Content-Security-Policy and security headers in vercel.json
description: The app renders sanitized HTML via dangerouslySetInnerHTML but has no CSP header, so if DOMPurify sanitization is ever bypassed, inline scripts execute with no browser-level backstop. vercel.json currently has no headers block. Add a headers block with: Content-Security-Policy (restrict script-src to self, img-src to self and Vercel Blob CDN), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY. This is defense-in-depth against the DOMPurify CVEs and any future sanitizer regression.

title: Lightbox zoom-in: main image blinks before final image appears
description: During the zoom-in animation for the Lightbox, the animations play fine and the lightbox appears correctly. However, during the zoom-in, the main image blinks (after the animation plays, but before final image-in-lightbox appears). Need to investigate root cause and add unit test to prevent regression.
notes:
Root cause: in useLightboxAnimations open effect, lightbox image reveal was awaited on Promise.all([wireframe(360ms), backdrop(360ms), sidebar(420ms)]). When wireframe finishes (360ms) it sets display:none, but the image isn't revealed until sidebar finishes (~60ms later). During that gap both are invisible → blink.

Fix: runWireframeAnimation now accepts an onAnimationEnd callback that fires after WAAPI animation.finished but before el.style.display='none'. Open effect uses it to reveal the lightbox image in the same microtask the wireframe is hidden.

PR: https://github.com/nikolai3d/fringematrix5/pull/74 (awaiting manual test before merge).

title: Lightbox: sidebar fade-in should only fire on initial zoom-in, not on prev/next navigation
description:
Reported by user: clicking PREVIOUS/NEXT (or pressing arrow keys, or swiping) inside an open lightbox replays the sidebar line→expand fade-in animation. This causes flickering on every navigation. The animation should run ONLY on the initial zoom-in from a thumbnail (or direct URL open), then stay settled for the rest of the lightbox session.

Root cause: the post-mount effect in useLightboxAnimations.ts has lightboxIndex in its deps array (needed for image-rect lookups), so it re-runs every navigation. The three sidebar('in') call sites inside that effect fire on every run.

Fix:
- Track a sidebarEnteredRef per open session.
- Reset to false in openLightbox (and on the reduceMotion fast path).
- Set to true after firing animateLightboxSidebar('in') the first time.
- Guard all three sidebar('in') call sites with the ref.
- Close path is unaffected — animateLightboxSidebar('out') still fires on close.

Tests:
- Source-level: assert the ref exists and that the in/out call sites are guarded.
- Behavior: mock Element.prototype.animate, render LightboxContainer with the hook, simulate open → navigate → close, count calls on the .lightbox-details element. Expect exactly 1 enter animation and 1 exit animation per session.
notes: Direct user follow-up to merged tweaks (PR #71). User wants to test before merge — keep this PR open until they confirm.

title: Lightbox redesign tweaks: remove on-image arrows, restore Download + Share in bottom toolbar
description:
Two tweaks to the just-shipped lightbox redesign:

1. Remove the side ◀/▶ arrow buttons that overlay the image edges. Per the updated concept, navigation lives only in the bottom toolbar. The side arrows are visually competing with the image and add a redundant code path.

2. Restore the DOWNLOAD and SHARE buttons in the bottom toolbar, sitting between PREVIOUS and NEXT (per the concept image). NEXT stays styled as the primary action.
   - DOWNLOAD: anchor with download attribute pointing at the current image src; left-icon ⬇ (or download glyph) + 'DOWNLOAD' label.
   - SHARE: button that uses navigator.share when available, falls back to clipboard.writeText with an alert. (This was the handleShare callback that was removed in vp3 — re-add it.)
   - Both styled as outline pills matching PREVIOUS (use .lightbox-nav-btn class, no primary modifier).

3. Update outside-click handler to remove the now-unused .lightbox-side-arrows zone.
4. Update outside-click handler to recognise the new buttons (they're inside .lightbox-nav-toolbar already, so they inherit that zone).
5. Update tests that asserted Download/Share were gone — invert those.
6. Update the e2e test 'Download and Share buttons are gone' — change to assert they ARE present and the URLs are correct.
7. CSS for the icons — small inline SVG or unicode glyph; lightweight.
notes: Direct follow-up to user feedback on the merged redesign (PRs #60-#70). The author bead fringematrix5-qyr remains separate.

title: Lightbox: Playwright e2e tests for new layout, nav, mobile drawer, reduce-effects
description:
Add e2e coverage in e2e/ (Playwright). Update existing tests that asserted Download/Share button presence.

New / updated test cases (e2e/lightbox-redesign.spec.ts):

1. Desktop layout:
   - Click a thumbnail → lightbox opens.
   - Assert .lightbox-details visible and contains IMAGE DETAILS heading.
   - Assert episode name text matches active campaign.
   - Assert SEASON / NUMBER row shows correct 'S4 · E08 (4.08)' style label.
   - Assert IMDB link href matches campaign.imdb_link.
   - Assert NO Download button, NO Share button visible.

2. Bottom nav buttons:
   - Click PREVIOUS → lightbox HUD index decrements.
   - Click NEXT → index increments.
   - Side ◀/▶ arrows still work (existing test — verify it still passes).

3. Keyboard:
   - ArrowRight / ArrowLeft / Escape still functional with new layout.

4. Mobile drawer (viewport <= 700px):
   - Sidebar not visible by default.
   - Info button toggles drawer open/closed.
   - Drawer closes on Escape, on outside tap, on close button.
   - Lightbox stays open when drawer closes.

5. Reduce-effects:
   - Set html.reduce-effects class via in-app toggle (or directly via page.evaluate).
   - Open lightbox; assert sidebar appears with NO animation classes / NO transient .lightbox-details-line element.
   - Close lightbox; same assertion in reverse.

6. Update existing e2e tests:
   - Any test asserting Download/Share presence — remove those assertions.

Use the existing playwright.config.ts (2 workers). Mark slow tests with test.slow() if they exceed default timeout.
notes: Existing e2e tests are in e2e/. Run with: npx playwright test lightbox-redesign.spec.ts

title: Lightbox: accessibility for redesigned view (focus, aria-live, sidebar updates)
description:
Ensure the redesigned lightbox is fully accessible.

Required:
- Sidebar has aria-live='polite' on the details container so screen readers announce the new episode info when the user navigates between images via PREVIOUS/NEXT/arrow keys/swipe.
- PREVIOUS / NEXT bottom buttons: visible text labels (already in concept); chevrons are decorative (aria-hidden='true').
- Existing side ◀/▶ arrows: keep aria-label='Previous' / 'Next' (already present).
- Close ✕ button: existing aria-label='Close' (already present).
- IMDB external link: include text-only suffix ' (opens in new tab)' as visually-hidden span, or use aria-label.
- Focus trap (useFocusTrap) must include all new interactive elements: bottom PREVIOUS, bottom NEXT, info button (mobile), IMDB link, hashtag link if present, close button, side arrows.
- Initial focus on lightbox open: close button (current behavior — verify still works after layout change).
- Focus return on close: thumbnail that was clicked (current behavior — verify still works).
- Tab order: image area → sidebar interactive elements (IMDB link, etc) → bottom toolbar → side arrows → close. Confirm reading order matches visual order.

Verify keyboard shortcuts still work without conflict:
- Escape closes lightbox.
- ArrowLeft/ArrowRight navigate.
- These should not trigger when focus is inside the IMDB link (let default link behavior happen) — currently they are document-level handlers; consider scoping or letting them through (no conflict expected since ArrowLeft/Right on a focused <a> does nothing).
notes: Existing focus trap hook is at client/src/hooks/useFocusTrap.ts. Add a snapshot of tabbable elements after layout change to make sure no orphan focus targets exist.

title: Lightbox: unit tests for parseEpisodeId + sidebar content rendering
description:
Add Vitest unit tests.

parseEpisodeId tests (client/test/parseEpisodeId.test.ts):
- '4.08' → { season: 4, episode: 8, label: 'S4 · E08 (4.08)' }
- '4.8'  → { season: 4, episode: 8, label: 'S4 · E08 (4.8)' }  (preserve original suffix verbatim)
- '5.13' → { season: 5, episode: 13, label: 'S5 · E13 (5.13)' }
- '' / null / 'garbage' → null
- '4.08.1' → null (or accept first two segments — pick a behavior and assert it)

Sidebar component tests (client/test/LightboxDetails.test.tsx or extend LightboxContainer test):
- Renders IMAGE DETAILS heading.
- Renders episode name, season/number label, air date, hashtag with leading '#', IMDB link with tt-id text.
- IMDB link has target='_blank' and rel='noopener noreferrer'.
- Does NOT render Download or Share buttons.
- Does NOT render an AUTHOR row (per scope decision).
notes: Existing test patterns are in client/test/. Use @testing-library/react. Mock the campaign prop in component tests.
close_reason: Unit tests delivered as part of fringematrix5-h65 (parseEpisodeId.test.ts) and fringematrix5-bqz (extractImdbId.test.ts + LightboxDetails.test.tsx).

title: Lightbox: new CSS for two-pane layout, details panel, nav toolbar, and reduce-effects rules
description:
Add and update styles in client/src/styles.css to match the concept.

New classes:
- .lightbox-layout — flex column container; top row (image+sidebar) and bottom row (nav toolbar).
- .lightbox-stage — flex row containing image + sidebar; centered horizontally with consistent gap.
- .lightbox-details — sidebar panel: dark surface, rounded corners (~12px), thin accent border, padding ~24px. Width ~320px on desktop. Position: relative (animation hook will override transform-origin).
- .lightbox-details-heading — IMAGE DETAILS label: cyan accent, uppercase, letter-spacing, underline rule below.
- .lightbox-details-row — vertical stack of label + value, with row gap.
- .lightbox-details-label — small uppercase, dim gray, letter-spacing.
- .lightbox-details-value — larger, foreground, monospace where appropriate (matches concept's mono feel).
- .lightbox-details-line — the thin horizontal line used during enter/exit animation; 2px height, accent color, full width.
- .lightbox-nav-toolbar — flex row at the bottom; gap; equal-weight children.
- .lightbox-nav-btn — pill button, transparent background, accent border, accent text, monospace label, uppercase, chevron icon. Hover: subtle glow.
- .lightbox-nav-btn--primary — solid accent fill, dark text.

Removals:
- Drop the .lightbox-actions selector (replaced by .lightbox-nav-toolbar).
- Drop styles tied to #download-btn and #share-btn.

Existing rules to revisit (line numbers from current styles.css):
- :407 .lightbox-actions { ... } — delete
- :1233 .lightbox img { ... } — verify still works in two-pane layout
- :1263 .lightbox-actions { ... } — delete
- :1273 .lightbox-brackets — confirm still positioned correctly relative to new layout
- :1325 .lightbox { ... } — may need flex-direction or grid adjustments
- :1414 'Simpler lightbox glow' (mobile media query) — confirm drawer styles play with it
- :1501 html.reduce-effects .lightbox img — add sibling rule for sidebar

Mobile breakpoint: add rules to hide .lightbox-details on small screens (the drawer task wires it back in via a toggle class).
notes: Use the existing CSS variables (--theme-accent, --theme-accent-rgb, --fg, --bg). Match font stack already used in .lightbox-hud for monospace rows.

title: Lightbox: respect reduce-effects / prefers-reduced-motion in sidebar animation
description:
The new sidebar enter/exit animation must be fully bypassed when reduce-effects is active. There are two existing signals:
- The 'reduceMotion' prop already flowing into useLightboxAnimations (driven by App.tsx state).
- The 'html.reduce-effects' class toggled in App.tsx (see App.tsx ~line 135) — used by styles.css rules at lines ~1495-1504.

Required behavior in reduce-effects mode:
- Sidebar appears instantly at full size with content visible (no line, no blink, no expand).
- Sidebar disappears instantly on close (no collapse animation).
- No Web Animations API calls fired for the sidebar in this mode.

CSS escape hatch:
- Add an 'html.reduce-effects .lightbox-details, html.reduce-effects .lightbox-details *' rule that disables all transitions/animations and forces opacity:1, transform:none.
- Also handle the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) media query for users who have not opted in via the in-app toggle.

JS escape hatch:
- In the new sidebar animation hook, branch on reduceMotion at the very top of openLightbox/closeLightbox (mirroring the existing pattern in useLightboxAnimations.ts lines 253-264 and 288-307).
notes: Touch points: useLightboxAnimations.ts (or new sibling hook), styles.css. Test coverage handled by the existing reduce-effects e2e/unit setup plus a new assertion in the lightbox e2e bead.

title: Lightbox: mobile collapsible drawer for IMAGE DETAILS sidebar
description:
On narrow viewports (existing mobile breakpoint, ~ <= 700px — confirm by reading styles.css), the sidebar should not occupy a side column. Instead:

- Sidebar is hidden by default on mobile.
- An 'i' info button appears on the lightbox toolbar (near the close button, or as a third element next to PREVIOUS/NEXT — pick the spot that does not crowd touch targets).
- Tapping the info button toggles the sidebar as an overlay/drawer covering ~80% of the screen (vertical slide-up from the bottom OR horizontal slide-in from the right — pick one and stay consistent).
- Tapping outside the drawer, the close (✕) button, or pressing Escape dismisses the drawer (without closing the lightbox).
- Drawer respects the sidebar enter/exit animation rules: same line-blink-expand on open, collapse on close. In reduce-effects mode the drawer just appears/disappears.

Accessibility:
- Info button has aria-label='Show image details' / 'Hide image details' (toggle).
- Drawer has role='dialog' and aria-modal='true' when open.
- Focus moves into the drawer on open, returns to the info button on close.
- Focus trap inside the drawer while open (reuse useFocusTrap if applicable).

Desktop (>= breakpoint): info button is hidden; sidebar is always visible alongside the image (no drawer).
notes: Confirm exact breakpoint by grep'ing 'max-width' in styles.css. The existing lightbox already disables body scroll while open; the drawer must not re-enable it.

title: Lightbox: sidebar enter/exit animation (line → expand, collapse → line)
description:
Implement the custom enter/exit animation for the new IMAGE DETAILS sidebar. Source timing values from the new lightbox.sidebarAnimation config block.

Enter sequence (when lightbox opens):
1. Sidebar starts as a thin horizontal line (height: ~2px, full panel width, vertical-center-aligned within where the full panel will be), visible immediately.
2. Line BLINKS lineBlinkCount times at lineBlinkIntervalMs intervals (opacity 1 ↔ 0).
3. After lineHoldMs, the line expands vertically (height grows to full panel height) over enterDurationMs - lineHoldMs, easing 'cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)'.
4. Inner content (IMAGE DETAILS heading + rows) fades in starting at contentFadeInDelayMs.

Exit sequence (when lightbox closes):
1. Inner content fades out.
2. Sidebar collapses vertically (height shrinks from full to ~2px line).
3. Line fades out.
4. Total duration = exitDurationMs.

Implementation approach: use Web Animations API (el.animate) like useLightboxAnimations does, OR pure CSS @keyframes + classes if simpler. The close path must complete BEFORE the lightbox unmounts — coordinate via Promise.all with the existing wireframe close animation so they finish together (whichever is longer governs the unmount).

Either extend useLightboxAnimations or create useLightboxSidebarAnimation alongside it. Expose a sidebarRef + animation state to LightboxContainer.
notes: Existing useLightboxAnimations already coordinates open/close via isAnimatingRef. The new sidebar animation must NOT extend the time before the user can interact — current LIGHTBOX_ANIM_MS is 360ms. Keep enter total close to that (~420ms is fine; do not exceed 600ms).

title: Lightbox: render IMAGE DETAILS sidebar content (episode / season / date / hashtag / IMDB)
description:
Populate the new
with the rows shown in the concept. The active Campaign object must be passed into LightboxContainer (currently it only gets images). Rows (in order): 1. EPISODE NAME → campaign.episode 2. SEASON / NUMBER → label from parseEpisodeId(campaign.episode_id) — e.g. 'S4 · E08 (4.08)' 3. AIR DATE → campaign.date (already a formatted string like 'January 13th, 2012'; render as-is) 4. HASHTAG → '#' + campaign.hashtag (also linkable to current campaign filter if cheap; otherwise plain text) 5. IMDB → campaign.imdb_link — render the title id parsed from the URL (e.g. tt2125636) as link text with an external-link icon next to it. target=_blank, rel='noopener noreferrer'. Markup pattern per row:
EPISODE NAME
Back To Where You've Never Been
Add IMAGE DETAILS heading at the top of the panel with the accent-cyan color and underline rule as in concept. AUTHOR row is intentionally OMITTED for now (filed as separate bead). Do not add a placeholder.
notes: Plumbing: App.tsx already has activeCampaignId + campaigns[]. Pass the resolved Campaign object (or campaign-by-id lookup) into LightboxContainer as a new prop. IMDB id extraction: a small parser like url.match(/\/(tt\d+)\//)?.[1] — fall back to the full URL as link text if parsing fails.

title: Lightbox: replace .lightbox-actions with new .lightbox-nav-toolbar (PREVIOUS / NEXT buttons)
description:
Add the bottom nav toolbar with two large pill-shaped buttons matching the concept:

- PREVIOUS button:
  - Class: .lightbox-nav-btn
  - Icon: left chevron, then label 'PREVIOUS'
  - aria-label='Previous image'
  - onClick: nextImage(-1)
- NEXT button:
  - Class: .lightbox-nav-btn .lightbox-nav-btn--primary
  - Label: 'NEXT', then right chevron
  - aria-label='Next image'
  - onClick: nextImage(+1)
  - Visually highlighted: solid accent (cyan) background, dark text — per concept.

Layout: flex row, equal spacing, takes full width below the image+sidebar pair. On wide viewports the two buttons occupy roughly the same horizontal extent as the image+sidebar combined (per concept).

DO NOT re-add Download or Share. Side ◀/▶ arrow buttons (.nav-btn) remain in their existing on-image positions.
notes: Buttons must stopPropagation on click so the backdrop click handler does not close the lightbox.
close_reason: Bundled into fringematrix5-vp3 — the new .lightbox-nav-toolbar with PREVIOUS/NEXT buttons (primary NEXT, aria-labels, stopPropagation, side arrows kept) was implemented as part of the layout scaffolding PR (#62). No separate work remained.

title: Lightbox: extend client/config.yaml with sidebarAnimation block
description:
Add a new build-time configuration block to client/config.yaml so the sidebar enter/exit animation timings are tunable without code changes:

lightbox:
  sidebarAnimation:
    enterDurationMs: 420       # total expand duration (line → full panel)
    exitDurationMs: 320        # total collapse duration (panel → line → gone)
    lineHoldMs: 120            # how long the horizontal line is visible before expanding
    lineBlinkCount: 2          # how many times the line blinks before expanding
    lineBlinkIntervalMs: 90    # blink interval
    contentFadeInDelayMs: 180  # when content inside the panel fades in (relative to enter start)

Add matching TypeScript types in client/src/config/ (alongside existing theme / loadingScreen config). Provide sensible defaults if the block is missing so the build does not break for older configs.

Document the new block briefly in CLAUDE.md under 'Key Implementation Details' (sidebar animation paragraph).
notes: vite-plugin-yaml already loads config.yaml at build time; pattern matches existing loadingScreen and theme blocks.

title: Lightbox: extract episode_id helper (parseEpisodeId)
description:
Add client/src/utils/parseEpisodeId.ts that converts a campaign episode_id like '4.08' into structured parts for the new sidebar.

Input: string in 'S.EE' form (e.g. '4.08', '5.13').
Output: { season: number, episode: number, label: string } where label is the human display form 'S4 · E08 (4.08)'.

Handle edge cases:
- Single-digit episode (e.g. '4.8') → still renders 'E08' (zero-padded to 2) and keeps original '(4.8)' suffix as-is.
- Malformed input → return null; sidebar row falls back to raw episode_id string.

Add Vitest unit tests in client/test/ covering happy path, zero-padding, malformed input.
notes: Used by the new IMAGE DETAILS sidebar (SEASON / NUMBER row). Pure function — no React dependencies.

title: Lightbox: restructure LightboxContainer to two-pane layout + bottom nav toolbar
description:
Rebuild LightboxContainer.tsx to match the concept layout.

Layout (desktop):
- Top region: flex row with two children:
  - Left: image container (existing #lightbox-image, keep current zoom animation).
  - Right: new <aside class='lightbox-details'> sidebar.
- Bottom region: new <div class='lightbox-nav-toolbar'> containing PREVIOUS and NEXT buttons (large, with chevron icons; NEXT styled as primary/accent per concept).
- Side ◀/▶ arrow buttons stay (overlay on image edges), per scoping decision.

Removals:
- Delete the Download anchor (<a id='download-btn'>).
- Delete the Share button (<button id='share-btn'>) and the handleShare callback.
- Delete the .lightbox-actions container (replaced by .lightbox-nav-toolbar).

Keep:
- #lightbox-close (✕ top-right).
- .lightbox-hud (FILE: name // index OF total) — verify it does not visually clash with the new layout; reposition if needed.
- Keyboard handlers (Escape / ArrowLeft / ArrowRight).
- Swipe handlers (pan-y, swipe-min thresholds).
- isAnimatingRef guard on backdrop clicks.

Update handleLightboxClick: 'click outside image or toolbar closes lightbox' must now also exclude clicks on the .lightbox-details sidebar and .lightbox-nav-toolbar.
notes: The sidebar content rendering is a separate bead — this one is layout scaffolding only. Use placeholder content inside .lightbox-details for now.

title: Epic: Redesign zoomed-in image view (details sidebar + bottom nav + custom sidebar animation)
description:
Redesign the lightbox (zoomed-in image view) to match the new concept: image on the left, IMAGE DETAILS sidebar on the right, PREVIOUS/NEXT buttons in a bottom toolbar. Remove the existing Download and Share buttons entirely. The sidebar has a custom enter/exit animation: on open it expands from a thin blinking horizontal line into the full panel; on close it collapses back to a line and disappears. Animation timings are customizable at build time via client/config.yaml. In reduce-effects mode the sidebar appears/disappears instantly with no animation.

Scope decisions (locked in during planning):
- Author field: OMIT for now (no data source). Filed as separate follow-up bead.
- Animation scope: sidebar ONLY. Main image keeps the existing thumbnail-zoom wireframe animation.
- Nav controls: ADD bottom PREVIOUS / NEXT buttons AND KEEP the existing side arrows.
- Mobile: sidebar becomes a collapsible drawer behind an 'i' info button on narrow viewports.

Current state files to touch:
- client/src/components/LightboxContainer.tsx  (layout, remove Download/Share)
- client/src/hooks/useLightboxAnimations.ts    (sidebar in/out animation hook or new sibling hook)
- client/src/styles.css                        (new lightbox layout classes, reduce-effects rules)
- client/config.yaml                            (new lightbox.sidebarAnimation block)
- client/src/config/                            (config schema/types)
- client/src/types/api.ts                       (if adding derived fields)
- client/src/utils/                             (new parseEpisodeId util)

Out of scope:
- AUTHOR field rendering (separate bead).
- Changes to thumbnail grid or main image zoom animation.
- New API endpoints (all data already exists in campaign + image).
notes: Concept image provided by user shows: IMAGE DETAILS header (cyan), then rows for EPISODE NAME, SEASON / NUMBER (S4 · E08 (4.08)), AIR DATE, HASHTAG (#CrossTheLine), IMDB (tt2125636 with external link icon), AUTHOR (avatar + @handle). Bottom toolbar has 4 buttons: PREVIOUS, DOWNLOAD, SHARE, NEXT — we are removing DOWNLOAD and SHARE.
close_reason: All sub-tasks completed across PRs #60–#70. AUTHOR row (fringematrix5-qyr) intentionally left open as a follow-up per the redesign scoping decision.

title: Detect collisions when deriving campaign ids
description: server/server.ts:158-172 deriveCampaignId produces one slug per campaign but does not check for duplicates across campaigns. Two campaigns with the same hashtag or icon_path would silently collapse to the same id; subsequent find-by-id lookups (e.g. /api/campaigns/:id/images) would always return the first one, making the second unreachable via deep link. Current data has no collisions (all entries use unique icon_path values), but a future yaml edit could introduce one silently. After mapping, scan for duplicate ids and throw at load time with the offending hashtag/icon_path in the error message. Surfaced in PR 40 review.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #53 — added duplicate id detection in loadCampaigns() with descriptive error message; 4 new tests

title: Extract useFocusTrap hook shared by ContentModal and settings modal
description: ContentModal.tsx:29-71 and App.tsx:490-529 (settings modal effect) are near-duplicates: same Escape handling, same Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, same querySelectorAll selector list. Extract into a useFocusTrap(active, modalRef, onClose) hook in client/src/hooks/ and use it in both places. Reduces drift risk if focusable-selector list or escape semantics ever change.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #54 — extracted useFocusTrap hook; ContentModal.tsx and App.tsx settings modal now use it

title: Resolve disabled-vs-abort inconsistency in campaign switch
description: App.tsx disables Home/Campaigns/nav arrows/sidebar items when isCampaignLoading is true (App.tsx:548-616), which prevents users from triggering the abort-on-switch logic added in fringematrix5-2du. The PR description claims 'the latest user action wins' via AbortController, but the disabled state blocks that path for non-cached loads. Pick one: drop disabled and rely on abort, OR keep disabled and remove the now-dead abort handling for user-driven switches (the abort still has value for the mount-load -> first-user-click overlap). Surfaced in PR 39 follow-up review.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #55 — kept disabled+abort design; added comment explaining why abort guards mount-load/first-click race

title: Test: error boundary fallback
description: Once the React error boundary exists, add a client test that throws inside a child and asserts the fallback renders instead of an unmounted tree.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #48 — added ErrorBoundary.test.tsx with 9 tests covering fallback rendering and normal child pass-through

title: Test: modal focus-trap contract
description: The focus management contract documented in client/src/App.tsx:52-62 (tab trap, close-button focus restoration) has no automated test. Add a Playwright or @testing-library/user-event test verifying tab cycles within the modal and focus returns to the trigger on close.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #51 — added modal-focus-trap.spec.ts with 7 Playwright tests covering the full focus contract

title: Test: malformed campaigns.yaml handling
description: Add a server test that loads a malformed/missing data/campaigns.yaml and asserts the server fails fast with a clear error rather than serving partial/garbage data.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #49 — added api.campaigns-yaml.test.ts with 10 tests covering ENOENT/YAML errors/malformed data

title: Test: blob API failure paths
description: Add server tests that simulate Vercel Blob returning errors (rate limit, 5xx, network failure) and assert the API responds correctly (proper status code, retry-after honored) — see server/server.ts:105-126.
close_reason: Addressed by PR 40: api.blob-errors.test.ts covers rate-limit (429+Retry-After), connection error (503), retry-with-non-rate-limit-error (503), and retry-success caching. Residual coverage gaps (avatars/* route, expired-cache-on-rate-limit) filed separately.

title: Replace handrolled .env.local parser with dotenv
description: server/server.ts:41-64 implements its own regex-based .env parser. Fails on escaped quotes, multiline values, and gives no type coercion. Switch to the dotenv package (likely already transitive).

title: Epic: Test coverage gaps
description: Tests cover happy paths but miss error scenarios: blob API failures, malformed campaigns.yaml, modal focus-trap contract documented in App.tsx, and error boundary fallback. Add coverage for failure modes.
close_reason: All 4 sub-tasks completed: blob API failure tests, focus-trap tests, malformed yaml tests, error boundary tests

title: Add unit tests for request timeout middleware
description: The 30-second request timeout middleware added in PR #77 (fringematrix5-4fg) has no unit test coverage. Add a Jest test using fake timers to verify: (1) the 503 response is sent after REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS, (2) res.locals['timedOut'] returns true after timeout fires, (3) the timer is cleared on response finish/close so it doesn't fire late. Existing tests in server/test/ can serve as patterns.

title: Add unit test for cache-hit state reset in selectCampaign
description: When navigating back to a cached campaign that previously had a load error, the campaignLoadError/Progress/Total state is now reset correctly (fixed in fringematrix5-luy). Add a unit or integration test in client/test/ that verifies: navigate to campaign A causing a load error, navigate to campaign B (which is cached), assert campaignLoadError is false and the error banner is not shown. This closes the regression-coverage gap noted in the fringematrix5-luy review.

title: Extract App.tsx God Component: factor out popovers, settings modal, and campaign loader hook
description: App.tsx is 828 lines owning all application state: campaigns, image loading, lightbox, sidebar, two popovers (Build Info, Share), three content modals, settings, accessibility prefs, loading progress, popover positioning, scroll/resize handlers, and all associated refs. The JSX renders toolbar, navbars, campaign info, gallery grid, and settings modal inline. Adding any new feature means editing this file. The popover positioning logic (lines 344-384) is self-contained. Fix in phases: (1) Extract Build Info and Share popovers to small components that own their positioning, taking isOpen/onClose/anchorRef as props. (2) Extract SettingsModal (see separate issue). (3) Factor loadCampaignImages and related state into a useCampaignLoader hook.

title: Move hardcoded production URL from App.tsx to client/config.yaml
description: App.tsx lines 387-389 hardcode 'https://fringematrix.art' and 'Check out Fringe Matrix' as the Threads share URL components. The production URL is not in client/config.yaml and cannot be overridden by environment variable. In staging/preview environments the share link always points to production. Fix: add a site.url (and optionally site.shareText) key to client/config.yaml and read it in the component. The existing config pipeline already handles this pattern.

title: Refactor useLightboxAnimations to accept getThumbElement callback instead of querying DOM by CSS class
description: useLightboxAnimations.ts queries the DOM directly using document.querySelector('.gallery-grid .card img[src=...]') and document.querySelectorAll('.gallery-grid .card img.lightbox-active-thumb') (lines 496-499, 688-691). It also appends wireframeElRef to document.body (line 172). These CSS class names are structural contracts: renaming gallery card markup or extracting a GalleryCard component silently breaks the lightbox animation with no compile error. Fix: pass a getThumbElement(index: number) => HTMLElement | null callback into the hook. The gallery grid, which already knows its own elements, supplies this via useCallback + useRef. This makes the selector logic testable and rename-safe.

title: Extract SettingsModal component from App.tsx to match ContentModal extraction pattern
description: The Settings modal (App.tsx lines 773-816, 44 lines of inline JSX) includes its own focus trap, two toggle rows, refs, and useFocusTrap integration — inlined directly in the 800-line App component. ContentModal, which is structurally similar (overlay + header + body + close button), was already extracted to components/ContentModal.tsx. There is no consistent rule for when UI gets its own component. Fix: extract SettingsModal following the ContentModal pattern. The reduceMotion/reduceEffects toggle callbacks remain in App.tsx since they affect html class and localStorage.

title: Unify ContentPage type between server.ts and client/src/types/api.ts
description: ContentPage = 'history' | 'credits' | 'legal' is independently defined in both server/server.ts (as a const tuple VALID_CONTENT_PAGES with derived type) and client/src/types/api.ts (as a standalone union type). Adding a new content page requires changes in both files plus potentially validate-config.js — a hidden sync requirement with no compile-time enforcement. Fix short-term: add cross-reference comments in both files. Fix long-term: create a shared/types.ts at the repo root and import it via path aliases on both sides.

title: Move FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR to module scope and deduplicate with useFocusTrap.ts version
description: FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR is defined inside the LightboxContainer component function body (line 43), so it is redeclared on every render. It also differs from the similar selector string in useFocusTrap.ts (line 3) — the LightboxContainer version includes :not([disabled]) guards while useFocusTrap.ts does not. A contributor reading both will not know whether the difference is intentional or a bug. Fix: move both to module scope; if the difference is intentional, extract to a shared focusable.ts utility with both variants documented.

title: Add RawCampaign interface for YAML parsing and fix pickNumber null type mismatch
description: Two related type-safety gaps: (1) deriveCampaignId(c: any, index) in server.ts (lines 248-261) and the map callback in loadCampaigns use c: any for raw YAML objects. Fields c.id, c.hashtag, c.icon_path are accessed but never typed. Fix: define a RawCampaign interface { id?: string; hashtag?: string; icon_path?: string; [key: string]: unknown } and use it. (2) pickNumber in client/src/config/lightbox.ts has parameter type 'number | undefined' but the body also guards against null (line 48). YAML can produce null for missing keys, so the declared type is wrong. Fix: change parameter type to 'number | undefined | null'.

title: Fix data:any type and expand deployedAt backward-compat comment in build-info handler
description: Two readability issues in the /api/build-info handler (server.ts lines 421-455): (1) The parsed JSON is typed as 'any' (line 425) instead of using the existing BuildInfo interface, forcing readers to mentally match accessed fields (data.repoUrl, data.commitHash, etc.) against the interface. Fix: use BuildInfo or Partial<BuildInfo> after parsing. (2) Line 434 has a comment 'backward compatibility' for the data.deployedAt fallback but gives no context on when deployedAt was used or replaced by builtAt. Fix: expand the comment to note the old field name and approximate when it was changed.

title: Extract duplicated cache-store logic in server.ts into a helper function
description: The three-line pattern of delete-before-set + LRU eviction + Map.set is copy-pasted identically for the initial fetch success path (server.ts lines 197-204) and the retry success path (lines 221-226). A developer adding a third fetch path would not know to replicate it again. Fix: extract into a cacheBlobResult(key, result) helper and call it from both locations.

title: Replace comma operator in useLightboxAnimations ternary with explicit if/else
description: useLightboxAnimations.ts line 629 uses the comma operator inside a ternary to both assign a ref and return a promise: (sidebarEnteredRef.current = true, animateLightboxSidebar('in')). This is valid JS but extremely uncommon in React codebases and stops most readers. Fix: replace with a straightforward if/else block that assigns sidebarEnteredRef.current = true then sets sidebarPromise = animateLightboxSidebar('in').

title: Rename images/imagesByCampaign state variables to clarify cache vs current-view semantics
description: In App.tsx, images (line 71) and imagesByCampaign (line 72) are too similar in name and don't communicate the distinction between 'currently displayed images' and 'loaded campaigns cache'. A new contributor cannot tell that images is always a mirror of imagesByCampaign[activeCampaignId] after loading, except during the load sequence. Fix: rename images to currentImages (or activeImages) and imagesByCampaign to imageCache (or loadedImagesByCampaign).

title: Memoize CampaignNavigation to prevent per-render closure allocation during loading
description: CampaignNavigation.tsx (lines 33-36) creates a new async closure per campaign button on every render. With 27 campaigns, 27 closures are allocated per App re-render. App re-renders every 400ms during isPreloading due to the loadingDots ticker, resulting in ~67 closure allocations per second during loading. Fix: wrap CampaignNavigation in React.memo — it only needs to re-render when campaigns, activeCampaignId, isOpen, or isCampaignLoading change. Optionally extract CampaignButton as a sub-component with a stable callback.

title: Replace querySelector DOM queries in handleLightboxClick with ref-based containment checks
description: handleLightboxClick in LightboxContainer.tsx (lines 89-101) runs up to 4 document.querySelector + getBoundingClientRect calls on every backdrop click via the isInsideZone helper. Each call forces a style recalculation/layout flush. On slow devices this delays the close animation start by a frame. Fix: use e.target.closest(selector) or ref.current.contains(e.target) instead. The drawerRef, infoBtnRef, and other elements are already available as refs or stable DOM nodes.

title: Wrap LightboxDetails in React.memo to prevent double re-computation on navigation
description: LightboxDetails is rendered twice in LightboxContainer.tsx — once in the inline sidebar (line 269) and once in the drawer (line 345). Both instances call parseEpisodeId and extractImdbId on every render. Neither instance is wrapped in React.memo. On every nextImage/prevImage action, both instances recompute even though the campaign prop doesn't change. Fix: wrap LightboxDetails in React.memo. The two-instance pattern scales poorly if details become richer.

title: Memoize gallery grid component to prevent re-renders on every loadingDots tick
description: App.tsx updates loadingDots state every 400ms while isPreloading is true (lines 448-450). The entire App component re-renders on each tick, and the gallery grid (images.map, lines 664-683) is defined inline with no memoization. For a campaign with 50+ images this recreates and diffs all N image card VDOM nodes at 2.5Hz for the duration of initial load (~12 wasted passes over 5 seconds). Fix: extract the gallery grid into a React.memo component, or wrap the gallery section in useMemo. Also see related issue: CampaignNavigation creates 27 new closures per tick.

title: Cache build-info.json parse at server startup instead of re-reading on every request
description: The GET /api/build-info handler (server.ts lines 421-455) runs fs.existsSync + fs.readFileSync + JSON.parse on every call. The file is immutable for the process lifetime. Fix: read and parse build-info.json once at startup into a module-level constant, and return the cached object in the handler.

title: Disable X-Powered-By header and add URL scheme validation for campaign href links
description: Two low-severity security hardening items: (1) Express emits X-Powered-By: Express on every response, fingerprinting the server stack. Fix: add app.disable('x-powered-by') near the top of server/server.ts. (2) Campaign links from campaigns.yaml (imdb_link, fringenuity_link, wiki_link) are injected directly into href attributes without URL scheme validation. Currently all values are developer-controlled https:// URLs, but if a javascript: URL ever lands in the YAML (compromised pipeline, careless edit), it executes on click. Fix: add a isSafeUrl helper that checks /^https?:\/\//i and gates all campaign href attributes.

title: Follow-up: add AUTHOR row to lightbox sidebar (needs author data source)
description:
The redesign concept includes an AUTHOR row in the IMAGE DETAILS sidebar (avatar circle with initials + @handle). This is OMITTED in the initial redesign because no author data currently exists in the model:
- campaigns.yaml has no author field.
- The /api/campaigns/:id/images endpoint returns only fileName + src.
- Blob filenames may or may not contain a parseable author handle (needs investigation).

Decision needed:
A) Parse handle from filename — only if filenames follow a strict convention. Pros: no schema change. Cons: brittle, depends on data hygiene.
B) New data/authors.yaml mapping path/filename → { handle, displayName, avatarUrl? }. Server merges into image objects. Most flexible.
C) Add author fields directly to data/campaigns.yaml at the per-image level (turns the yaml schema from per-campaign to per-image, big change). Probably overkill.

When picked up:
- Investigate filename samples to determine if (A) is viable.
- Add new fields to ApiImageData and ImageData types.
- Render AUTHOR row in .lightbox-details with avatar circle (initials from handle) + @handle text.
- Add tests for the chosen pipeline.
notes: Blocked on data-source decision. Bead intentionally created with no dependency so it can be picked up independently once the author pipeline is sorted.

title: Lightbox: remove Download/Share handlers and dead code
description:
After the layout refactor lands, clean up:
- handleShare callback in LightboxContainer.tsx (lines ~37-54).
- Any unused imports (e.g. navigator.share/clipboard references) tied to it.
- styles for #download-btn, #share-btn, .action-btn in styles.css (search for these selectors and verify they are not used elsewhere).
- Any e2e or unit tests that assert presence of Download or Share buttons — update or remove.

Search command to run before declaring done:
  grep -rn 'download-btn\|share-btn\|action-btn\|handleShare' client/ server/ e2e/
notes: Low-priority cleanup. Do NOT do this until the new toolbar is shipped — keep the diff focused.

title: Reconsider 'No token found' string-match fallback in listBlobsWithCache
description: server/server.ts:158-161 catches errors whose message includes 'No token found' and returns an empty blob list. The HAS_BLOB_TOKEN check at line 111 already handles the legitimate fallback case (token unset). The string-match exists for the scenario where BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN is set but invalid, but treating that as 'empty list' silently hides real config errors in production. Consider removing the string match so an invalid token surfaces as 503 BlobUnavailableError instead. Validate with a deliberate-invalid-token integration test before changing.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #56 — removed 'No token found' string-match fallback; invalid tokens now surface as 503

title: Test coverage: content cache TTL and eviction
description: server/server.ts:358-383 implements TTL (5 min) and a size-cap eviction policy (CONTENT_CACHE_MAX = 16, evicts oldest by insertion order). Neither path has a test. With only 3 valid content pages today the eviction branch is unreachable in practice, but future expansion of VALID_CONTENT_PAGES would silently regress the policy if untested. Add tests that: (1) cache hit returns within TTL, (2) cache miss after TTL re-reads file, (3) eviction removes oldest entry when capacity is exceeded. May require Date.now() mocking or a smaller CONTENT_CACHE_MAX via dependency injection.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #47 — added api.cache.test.ts covering TTL expiry and LRU eviction; also added BLOB_CACHE_MAX constant and proper LRU logic to server.ts

title: Test coverage: /avatars/* route
description: The /avatars/* redirect route (server/server.ts:233-263) is entirely uncovered by tests. PR 40 added BlobUnavailableError -> 503 mapping to this route but no test exercises it. Add at least: (1) 302 redirect on found blob, (2) 404 when blob not present, (3) 503 when Vercel Blob is unavailable. Use the same jest.unstable_mockModule pattern from api.blob-errors.test.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #46 — added api.avatars.test.ts covering 302/404/503 cases for /avatars/* route

title: Document or fix mount-effect empty deps with loadCampaignImages
description: App.tsx mount effect (lines 396-441) calls loadCampaignImages from inside an async IIFE but declares deps as []. loadCampaignImages is a stable useCallback([]) so behavior is correct, but react-hooks/exhaustive-deps would flag it. Either add a one-line comment explaining the intentional empty deps, or include loadCampaignImages in the array (safe because it's stable). Pre-emptive cleanup before any future lint config change.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #42 — added loadCampaignImages to mount effect deps array (safe, stable useCallback)

title: Decide fate of activeModalRef now that openModal uses AbortController
description: openModal in App.tsx aborts the prior controller and checks signal.aborted at every await boundary. The activeModalRef.current === page guards on lines 308/316/320 are mostly redundant with that abort check. Comment calls them 'defense in depth'. Either remove activeModalRef, OR add a clarifying comment explaining the one branch it covers that the signal does not (state mutation in a non-aborted-but-superseded scenario, if any actually exists). Surfaced in PR 39 follow-up review.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #50 — removed activeModalRef entirely (proven redundant with signal.aborted)

title: Guard localStorage access for SSR safety
description: client/src/App.tsx:89, 104 access localStorage in effects without a typeof window guard. Not a bug today (CSR-only) but blocks adding SSR. Use lazy init with the typeof window check.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #43 — added typeof window guards before localStorage accesses in App.tsx

title: Replace catch (error: any) with typed errors
description: server/server.ts uses 'catch (error: any)' throughout, accessing .message/.name/.retryAfter without guards. Introduce a small typed error hierarchy (BlobRateLimitError, BlobUnavailableError, ...) so retry/backoff logic is auditable.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #57 — changed all catch(error: any) to catch(error: unknown) with proper type narrowing; added BlobRateLimitedError interface and helper functions

title: Enable TypeScript strict mode
description: Both client/tsconfig.json and server/tsconfig.json are not in strict mode; 'any' types appear in server.ts and App.tsx. Enable strict: true incrementally (start with noImplicitAny + strictNullChecks) and fix fallout.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #58 — both tsconfigs already had strict:true; fixed 25 latent type errors in 7 server test files

title: Extract magic numbers to named constants
description: client/src/App.tsx:727 (swipe thresholds 50px/75px/500ms) and client/src/hooks/useLightboxAnimations.ts:27-30 (retry frames, 500ms timeout) use unexplained literals. Pull into named constants with a one-line comment explaining the choice.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #52 — added individual JSDoc comments to swipe threshold constants in LightboxContainer.tsx

title: Add pagination for blob listings
description: server/server.ts:234 uses limit: 1000 for blob listing. Fine today, but campaigns with thousands of images will OOM the response. Add cursor pagination on the server endpoint and stream/page on the client.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #59 — added cursor pagination loop in listBlobsWithCache with MAX_BLOB_ITEMS=10000 cap; 4 new pagination tests

title: Archive or update stale top-level docs
description: DEPLOYMENT_STATUS.md and VERCEL_BUILD_DEBUG.md look like migration-era debug artifacts referencing old 401/protection issues. Either update to current state or move to docs/archive/ (or delete).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #45 — deleted DEPLOYMENT_STATUS.md, VERCEL_BUILD_DEBUG.md, VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT.md (stale migration artifacts)

title: Gitignore ai_logs/ and remove from repo
description: ai_logs/ contains ~2.3 MB of Cursor conversation logs checked into the repo. Bloats deploys and clones. Add to .gitignore and remove via git rm.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #44 — added ai_logs/ to .gitignore and removed 13 log files from git tracking

title: Epic: Repo hygiene & polish
description: Cleanup of stale docs, checked-in logs, magic numbers, loose TypeScript config, and hydration guards. Lower-priority quality-of-life improvements.
close_reason: All 7 sub-tasks completed: pagination, magic numbers, strict TS, gitignore logs, localStorage guards, stale docs, typed errors

title: Move isInsideZone to module scope in LightboxContainer and extract repeated data.campaigns expression in App.tsx
description: Two structural readability issues: (1) LightboxContainer.tsx lines 89-103: isInsideZone helper is defined inside handleLightboxClick which is inside useCallback, creating 3 levels of nesting. The helper cannot be tested or reused. Fix: move to module scope as isInsideSelector(selector, x, y). (2) App.tsx ~line 413: data.campaigns || [] is repeated 4 times in 7 lines in the initial campaign selection logic even though data.campaigns was already safely guarded when stored into state. Fix: extract const campaignList = data.campaigns || [] before the block and use it consistently.

title: Add summary comments to 85-line animation useEffect and 3-tier thumbnail fallback in useLightboxAnimations
description: Two dense sections in useLightboxAnimations.ts lack orientation comments: (1) The single useEffect on isLightboxOpen (lines 566-651) is 85 lines handling 4 distinct code paths: no-animation (reduceMotion), no thumbnail rect (sidebar-only), zero-dimension rect fallback, and full wireframe+backdrop+sidebar. Add a comment block before the rAF callback naming the four paths. (2) Lines 496-512 have 3 cascading fallbacks for thumbnail element lookup (CSS selector, activeGridThumbRef, lastOpenedThumbElRef) with no explanation of the precedence order or why each fallback is less preferred. Add a comment explaining the cascade.

title: Add comments explaining src/loadedSrc two-phase model and latestOpenStateRef sync pattern
description: Two unexplained patterns in App.tsx: (1) Lines 178-195: placeholder ImageData objects have src:null, isLoading:true, loadedSrc:null, then are replaced with fully loaded objects where both src and loadedSrc are set. The gallery renders img.loadedSrc || img.src — the reason loadedSrc exists alongside src is never explained. Add a comment near the placeholder creation. (2) Lines 346-354: three useEffect calls + a useRef keep latestOpenStateRef in sync. The reason (keeping onScrollOrResize stable without triggering a new addEventListener cycle on each toggle) is attached to a later effect, not to the sync effects themselves. Add a comment above both sync effects.

title: Clear modalLoadAbortRef.current after abort in closeModal
description: closeModal (App.tsx:328-340) calls modalLoadAbortRef.current?.abort() but leaves the ref pointing at the aborted controller. Functionally harmless (GC'd next openModal call), but inconsistent with the pattern elsewhere and a minor code-smell. Set the ref to null after abort to match.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #41 — added modalLoadAbortRef.current = null after abort() in closeModal
